rulings.Case Title: BETTY UTTERBACK VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 5, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Campbell Soup Company¿s motion for summary judgment is continued one more time on the court¿s own motion to April 6. The court has not concluded its review of the out-of-state case law bearing on the issue of whether defendant owed a duty to Betty Utterback. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD HELLEN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Substitute Successor For Deceased Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 5, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiff¿s motion to substitute Maria Sablan as successor-in-interest for deceased plaintiff Richard Hellen is denied without prejudice to being refiled once the status of nominal defendant LeAnn Helen is resolved and, if she remains a party in the case, she is served with the motion. Moreover, any refiled motion should clear up the uncertainty in the moving papers whether the decedent as to whom a successor needs to be appointed is Richard Hellen or Karen Hellen. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KIM MORGAN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 5, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1, 4 and 5 (negligence, premises owner/contractor liability and punitive damages) and taken off calendar as to issues 2 and 3 (strict liability and false representation). As to negligence and premises owner/contractor liability, plaintiff¿s deposition and declaration create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing pipe insulation attributable to defendant. As to punitive damages, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain clear and convincing evidence that defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression warranting the imposition of punitive damages. Defendant¿s purported undisputed facts no. 44-47 fail to demonstrate that plaintiff submitted factually devoid responses to defendant¿s discovery seeking all facts, witnesses and documents supporting plaintiff¿s punitive damages claim, precluding defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff dismissed his claims for strict liability and false representation. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ART HOOVER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 5, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant Oakfabco, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products attributable to defendant. By failing to demonstrate in its separate statement that defendant propounded interrogatories on plaintiff seeking all facts, witnesses and documents in support of plaintiff¿s claims against it and by failing to demonstrate that plaintiff¿s responses thereto were factually devoid, defendant cannot successfully invoke the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Further, purported undisputed fact no. 4 states that plaintiff only identified himself and Mr. Hagele as witnesses as to defendant, and cites to its special interrogatory no. 1. However, defendant¿s interrogatory no. 1 did not ask for the identity of persons with knowledge of facts supporting plaintiff¿s claims against defendant and plaintiff¿s response to no. 1 identified two co-workers (Mr. Cuddihy and Mr. Chacon) who are not mentioned in defendant¿s separate statement. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain clear and convincing evidence that defendant acted with malice, oppression or fraud to warrant punitive damages. Defendant¿s separate statement does not even mention this issue. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WANDA SIMPSON et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Action For Delay In Prosecution And Failure To Bring Action To Trial

rulings.Case Title: PETER MCKEON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
